title: 9 Sending Bonds/Coupons to Central 1 on Collection
body_text:
9	Sending Bonds/Coupons to Central 1 on Collection
9.1	Introduction to Sending Bonds/Coupons to Central 1 on Collection
This Chapter provides procedures for redeeming Government of Canada Coupons, Coupon Bonds, Marketable Bonds, and other types of bonds that require manual handling on collection. The processing steps for these types of bonds/coupons are as follows:
Prepare the deposit for Central 1 (there is no need to include a deposit slip).
Notify Central 1 via ServiceNow of the bonds/coupons to be sent on collection.
Forward the original bonds/coupons to Central 1 per the internal credit union transportation policy.
9.2	Preparing the Deposit for Central 1
When preparing a deposit for Central 1, the bonds must be part of a separate Canadian deposit coded â00â. This deposit wonât be successful if Ontario Savings Bonds are present in the deposit. At the end of the business day, proceed as follows:
1.
Make a photocopy of each bond/coupon.
2.
Create a separate tape listing for the values of each type of bond/coupon, if applicable.
3.
Attach the tape listing to the applicable type of bonds/coupons.
4.
Complete a Redeeming Bonds on Collection notification on ServiceNow and attach images of coupons/bonds to the bonds ticket. See Section 9.3, Notifying Central 1 of Bonds Sent on Collection, for procedures.
5.
Place the bonds/coupons with the attached tape listings and powers of attorney (if applicable) in an envelope.
6.
Record the ServiceNow bonds task number on the envelope. See Section 9.3, Notifying Central 1 of Bonds Sent on Collection.
7.
Record the credit union transit number on the top left-hand corner of the envelope.
8.
Stamp the envelope with the credit unionâs Endorsement Stamp.
9.
Forward the envelope as per the internal credit union transportation policy by courier to:
Central 1 Credit Union
Payment Services
Attention: Tracing
1441 Creekside Drive
Vancouver, BC V6J 4S7
Important
:	It is the credit unionâs responsibility to ensure that deposits can be reconstructed if they are lost or destroyed in transit. The credit union must retain copies of all bonds forwarded to Central 1. Ensure backup records are retained in the same order as originally submitted.
9.3	Notifying Central 1 of Bonds Sent on Collection
Credit unions must notify Central 1 of bonds/coupons sent to Central 1 on collection. The procedures in this Section describe the notification process via ServiceNow.
Note:	If ServiceNow is unavailable, complete Redeeming Bonds on Collection, Form 6139, and email a copy to
tracers1@central1.com
. Send the original form to Central 1 with the bonds.
To complete Redeeming Bonds on Collection, Form 6139, on ServiceNow, proceed as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications.
See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ServiceNow.
See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 3: Accessing ServiceNow
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select Service Catalogue
Select
Paper Payments
, and then select
Redeeming Bonds on Collection
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Paper Payments â Redeeming Bonds on Collection
Complete the Redeeming Bonds on Collection page as follows. See Figure 6.
â
Enter the phone number.
â¡
Use the drop-down arrow to select Method of Transportation.
â¢
Enter the Tracking Number.
â£
Select the Type of Bonds (multiple types can be selected).
â¤
Enter the corresponding dollar values.
â¥
Enter any additional comments.
â¦
Attach scanned copies of the bonds to be redeemed.
â§
Select
Submit
.
Figure 6: Redeeming Bonds on Collection Form
The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created.
Important
:	Record the ServiceNow ticket number on the envelope that contains the original bonds to be sent to Central 1. Make sure the number and types of original bonds in the envelope match the images attached to the Bonds ticket in ServiceNow.
Users can edit the Bonds ticket as shown in Figure 7:
â
Select the External Watch List icon to add/remove stakeholders.
â¡
Select the Additional Comments field to add comments.
â¢
Select the paperclip icon to manage attachments.
â£
Select
Save
to confirm changes.
Figure 7: Editing Options in the Bonds Ticket
9.4	Settlement and Adjustment for Bonds/Coupons Sent on Collection to Central 1
Central 1 will settle daily for redeemed bonds through the credit unionâs Central 1 Canadian Dollar current account. Central 1 settles for the amount in the redemption value box.
If the incorrect amount is written in the redemption value box, the credit union will not be credited immediately for the correct amount. The Bank of Canada will pick up the error and process the required adjustment, but this may take up to a month. The minimum amount for adjustments must be greater than $6.99. The Bank of Canada will not
process adjustments on amounts of $6.99 or less.
END
